content:
🔥 viewermetrics | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Analyzing live stream viewership to identify and filter out bot accounts, providing streamers with accurate audience metrics.
✨ Key Features: • Track viewer counts and authenticated users • Detect potential bot accounts • View detailed user profiles • Identify suspicious account creation patterns • Automatic adaptation to channels
📖 Summary: The Viewer Metrics Chrome Extension tracks and analyzes live stream viewers, differentiating between genuine users and potential bot accounts. It provides detailed analytics, user profiles, and visual charts to help streamers understand their audience composition.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 dock-droid | Dockerfile
🎯 Primary Use Case: Running and testing Android environments within Docker containers for development, security research, and CI/CD pipelines.
✨ Key Features: • Run QEMU Android x86 and ARM in Docker • X11 Forwarding • ADB on port :5555 • Magisk, riru, LSPosed support on Android x86 • SSH enabled
📖 Summary: Dock-Droid allows users to run Android x86 and ARM environments within Docker containers using QEMU and KVM. It facilitates advanced use cases like security research, CI/CD pipelines, and app testing with features like X11 forwarding, ADB access, and GPU passthrough.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ socktop | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Remote system monitoring with a user-friendly TUI.
✨ Key Features: • Remote monitoring via WebSocket (JSON over WS) • Optional WSS (TLS) with client certificate pinning • TUI built with ratatui • Detailed CPU, Memory, Swap, Disk, Network, and Temperature metrics • Sortable process table with scrollbars
📖 Summary: Socktop is a remote system monitoring tool that provides a rich TUI interface, inspired by `top` and `btop`. It communicates with a lightweight agent over WebSockets, offering detailed system metrics and process information.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 Cronicle | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: A distributed task scheduler and runner with a web-based UI, serving as an advanced replacement for traditional cron jobs across multiple servers.
✨ Key Features: • Single or multi-server setup • Automated failover to backup servers • Auto-discovery of nearby servers • Real-time job status with live log viewer • Plugins can be written in any language
📖 Summary: Cronicle is a robust, multi-server task scheduler and runner written in Node.js, offering a web-based UI for managing scheduled and on-demand jobs. It provides features like automated failover, real-time job status, live log viewing, and the ability to write plugins in any language.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 web-llm | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To enable high-performance, privacy-preserving LLM inference directly within web browsers, leveraging local hardware acceleration (WebGPU) and offering OpenAI API compatibility for a wide range of open-source models.
✨ Key Features: • In-Browser Inference • Full OpenAI API Compatibility • Structured JSON Generation • Extensive Model Support
📖 Summary: WebLLM is a high-performance inference engine that runs Large Language Models (LLMs) directly in web browsers using WebGPU for hardware acceleration. It offers full compatibility with the OpenAI API, enabling local execution of various open-source models with features like streaming and JSON mode, all without requiring server support.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
